channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - techraptor.net
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for techraptor.net

item.title: The First Pokemon Pokopia Limited-Time Event Will Let You Befriend Hoppip
item.content:encoded:
Nintendo has revealed the first in a line of post-launch limited-time events coming to Pokemon Pokopia, and this one will give you the facility to recruit and befriend a Generation 2 Grass/Flying trio.

As outlined in a news post, the first Pokemon Pokopia limited-time event will begin on March 9th, which is just four days after the game's release. It'll introduce a new resource, Cotton, which should give you a clue as to which Pokemon will be added.

The event, "More Spores for Hoppip", will, as you've probably guessed by now, give you the chance to make friends with Grass/Flying-type Hoppip, as well as its evolved forms Skiploom and Jumpluff.

As well as the three aforementioned Pokemon, you'll also be able to obtain "picnic-themed furniture items" by gathering cotton spores, and creating new habitats with these items will stand you in good stead with Skiploom and Jumpluff.

You'll need to have rebuilt your Pokemon Center in Pokemon Pokopia to take advantage of this event, and the three 'mons won't be available outside of the event either, so make sure that you befriend them while you can. It runs until March 24th.

If you're not familiar with Pokemon Pokopia, it's a life sim making its way to Nintendo Switch 2 tomorrow, and it casts you as an anthropomorphic Ditto attempting to rebuild the ruins of an island in order to attract Pokemon friends to live there with you.

In order to do so, you'll have to make sure your island is palatable to them, Viva Pinata-style, and you'll have access to a host of other minigames and fun life sim-style systems as well. In short, it's the game you've probably been waiting for if you're looking to play a calmer, more slow-paced Pokemon adventure.

Pokemon Pokopia launches exclusively for Nintendo Switch 2 on March 5th. Stay tuned for more.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph



https://techraptor.net/gaming/news/pokemon-pokopia-limited-time-event-hoppip
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ravenswatch Roadmap for 2026 and 2027 Promises New DLC and More
item.content:encoded:
Developer Passtech Games and publisher Nacon have released a brand new Ravenswatch roadmap, and it's chock-full of stuff coming to the action roguelike over the next year or so.

In a Steam post, the two studios first express pride in completing "one full year of post-launch support" for the game before promising another year of updates and DLC to look forward to.

At the moment, the roadmap is a little barebones; Passtech and Nacon say they will reveal more about each upcoming update and DLC pack as release draws nearer, so for now, you'll have to content yourself with a brief overview of what's in store for Ravenswatch.

The first major update that's coming to Ravenswatch in 2026 is Songs of Thieves, which will, as the name suggests, bring the Thieves faction to the game. You can also look forward to new room types, a new NPC, and a paid skin pack if you're the type who likes to admire your heroes.

Following that, a new hero will be introduced as part of a second paid DLC pack, with a talent rework also being included in a minor free update. All of this will be followed by updates introducing new game modes and quality-of-life changes, as well as more paid DLC.

News of Ravenswatch's second year of content comes after publisher Nacon delayed its upcoming Nacon Connect presentation to May, which itself follows the publisher filing for insolvency just days prior.

Nacon's desire to continue supporting Ravenswatch makes a lot of sense in this environment, too; the game is the publisher's second most popular on Steam by player count (at time of writing).

You can check out Ravenswatch right now on PC, PlayStation, Xbox, and Nintendo Switch (although there's still no native Switch 2 version, unfortunately). Stay tuned for more on this and all other things Nacon.

Joe has been writing for TechRaptor for several years, and in those years has learned a lot about the gaming industry and its foibles. He’s originally an… More about Joseph
